questions.q: What is the standard electrode potential of hydrogen electrode?
questions.year_pattern: Typical board year pattern this resembles (e.g., '2019-style')

questions.q: What is the function of a salt bridge in a galvanic cell?
questions.answer: A salt bridge is used to connect the two half-cells and to maintain electrical neutrality.

questions.q: Calculate the emf of the cell: Zn(s) | Zn2+(aq) || Cu2+(aq) | Cu(s) when [Zn2+] = 0.01 M and [Cu2+] = 0.001 M.
What is the difference between an anode and a cathode in an electrochemical cell?
questions.answer: An anode is the electrode where oxidation occurs, while a cathode is the electrode where reduction occurs.

questions.q: What is the role of a electrolyte in an electrochemical cell?
questions.answer: An electrolyte is a substance that helps in the conduction of electricity in the cell.

questions.q: Calculate the number of moles of electrons passed during the electrolysis of 1.2 L of 0.1 M H2SO4. (Atomic mass of H = 1 g/mol, O = 16 g/mol)
questions.answer: Moles of electrons passed = moles of H2 produced
